items.title: Pair found guilty of killing elderly man during meth-addled burglary

children.content: Two men are convicted of killing 68-year-old Raymond Smith in his own home in Western Australia's South West during a meth-addled burglary for drugs.

gallery.altText: A man with glasses and his hair in a ponytail looks away from the camera.
A court sketch of a dark haired man with glasses pictured beside a blonde man

thumbnail.altText: A man with glasses and his hair in a ponytail looks away from the camera.
A court sketch of a dark haired man with glasses pictured beside a blonde man

items.title: Teen's 5-day wait in hospital emergency department prompts official apologies

image.altText: The exterior of a regional hospital building

children.content: The WA health minister and director of the state's country health service have unreservedly apologised to a 17-year-old left in an emergency room for 116 hours waiting for specialised mental health care.

items.title: Docker O'Driscoll says mental health admission 'best thing' he's done

children.content: Fremantle Dockers midfielder Nathan O'Driscoll says he has received "nothing but love" after posting on social media about his struggles with anxiety and how he contemplated taking his own life.

image.altText: Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll celebrates a goal with a shout in front of fans at Perth Stadium, in Fremantle's white jersey.

items.title: Medical clinics closing down 'like dominoes' with new bulk-billing model

children.content: Kalbarri residents will be forced to travel more than 100 kilometres to visit a doctor when the regional WA town's only GP clinic closes due to financial pressure.

image.altText: Image of Monica wearing a t shirt and her hair tied back. She stands in front of the ocean and looks concerned at camera.

items.title: Strongest storm in half a century caps off your top WA weather photos for May

children.content: Bursts of sunshine, brief spells of rain and long stretches of dry skies across May left many West Australians wondering when winter would finally arrive — until it did with a bang. Take a look back in pictures.

gallery.altText: A silhouette of teenage girls playing volleyball on the beach at sunset
Mist settles over a trees in an image with blue hues
Waves crash over Sugarloaf Rock with whitewater spraying into the air.

thumbnail.altText: A silhouette of teenage girls playing volleyball on the beach at sunset
Mist settles over a trees in an image with blue hues
Waves crash over Sugarloaf Rock with whitewater spraying into the air.

items.title: Pilot 'unsure' which button was pressed before plane crashed in Broome

children.content: Australia's transport safety watchdog is investigating whether a pilot inadvertently turned a plane's engine off before it crashed in WA's north earlier this year, with five passengers and two pilots on board.

image.altText: A cessna plane wreckage with writing overtop of picture

items.title: Copper thieves are more brazen and taking exceptional risks, demolition experts say

children.content: Demolition sites are being stripped for copper at an escalating rate, according to a Perth demolition contractor, risking electrocution and asbestos exposure to sell the scrap metal on the black market.

items.title: Woman credits 'divine intervention' after tree hits car in huge WA storm

children.content: A mother shares her incredible tale of survival after a tree falls on her car in a freak accident on a South West highway during WA's strongest storm in 49 years.

articles.title: Strongest storm system in 49 years tears through Perth, southern WA

children.content: Tens of thousands of homes are left without power and the SES receives hundreds of calls for help, with the roofs torn off two apartment buildings, as WA's southern half is hammered by more severe weather.

image.altText: A badly damaged apartment block with its roof torn off and metal debris.

gallery.altText: A car with significant damage parked on the side of the road at night
A damaged car on the side of a highway.

thumbnail.altText: A car with significant damage parked on the side of the road at night
A damaged car on the side of a highway.

items.title: These are the 'worst offenders' in Australian packaging

children.content: A trendy plastic-and-metal takeaway cup used by cafes for iced drinks has been named Australia's worst packaging in the first Unpackit Awards.

image.altText: Four dirty, empty can-shaped plastic drinking vessels with a metal pull tab top.

items.title: Three used submarines a 'better arrangement' for Australia, minister says

children.content: The defence minister says the new AUKUS deal that will see Australia buy three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the US is what the government had wanted all along. Follow live.

image.altText: Richard Marles speaks during an AUKUS Defence ministers' press huddle

items.title: 'Extraordinary career': Hundreds farewell veteran MP at state funeral

children.content: Hundreds of mourners farewell WA's longest-serving parliamentarian, war hero Bill Grayden, at his state funeral in Perth.

gallery.altText: A portrait of Bill Grayden on a table during a state funeral.
Pallbearers in military outfits carry a casket draped in an Australian flag up a cathedral aisle, with mourners either side.
Kokoda campaign veteran Bill Grayden at a service in Kings Park.

thumbnail.altText: A portrait of Bill Grayden on a table during a state funeral.
Pallbearers in military outfits carry a casket draped in an Australian flag up a cathedral aisle, with mourners either side.
Kokoda campaign veteran Bill Grayden at a service in Kings Park.

items.title: Banned products in Australia sold on Temu, Amazon and AliExpresss

children.content: Products that may already be banned in Australia are being sold by retail giants including Amazon, Temu, AliExpress and eBay.

image.altText: A bunch of toys, including fake cigarettes displayed with a white background

items.title: The housing 'super cycle' may be ending, and that could be a good thing

children.content: Before the federal budget, the housing market was already starting to choke on its own excesses. Now some economists are tipping an extended period where home prices grow no faster than general inflation.

image.altText: A Queenslander house with an open home sign.

items.title: Grieving mum's experience highlights need for regulation in funeral sector

children.content: A First Nations family is calling for changes to the "unregulated" funeral industry after complaints about a loved one's burial went nowhere.

gallery.altText: A woman unsmiling with grey hair.

thumbnail.altText: A woman unsmiling with grey hair.

items.title: Green top-scores and Ellis stands tall as Australia levels ODI series

children.content: Nathan Ellis shines with the ball, while Cameron Green tops the run-scoring for Australia in a 41-run ODI victory over Pakistan in Lahore.

image.altText: Cameron Green cuts a cricket ball away as Pakistan's wicketkeeper looks on.

items.title: Renegades BBL team to be sold as new Victorian franchise established

children.content: The Melbourne Renegades BBL licence is set to be sold to a private investor, with Cricket Victoria to establish a new team, marking the end of the Stars as well.

image.altText: a batter dressed in green hits the ball, while a keeper in red is behind him at the stumps

items.title: This rapper was having panic attacks on stage, until he transitioned

children.content: Poet, rapper, musician, playwright and novelist Kae Tempest is a prodigious polymath. It wasn't until he affirmed his gender that he connected with his true self.

image.altText: Close-up of Kae Tempest looking down at sunset, with a city skyline in silhouette behind them.

items.title: Fertiliser imports hit 1 million tonne mark since Iran war began

children.content: Streamlined biosecurity checks of fertiliser shipments have fast-tracked imports of urea for Australia's winter crop.

image.altText: Container ships docked at Port Botany.

items.title: Spotlight on Welcome to Country ceremonies in wake of Anzac Day controversy

children.content: Indigenous elders explain why they believe Welcome to Country ceremonies have faced opposition, and why abandoning the practice would create a cultural "abyss".

gallery.altText: An Indigenous man in traditional cultural dress at a smoking ceremony
Noongar elder Aunty Tina Hayden carries out a Welcome to Country
Children watch a welcome to country ceremony

thumbnail.altText: An Indigenous man in traditional cultural dress at a smoking ceremony
Noongar elder Aunty Tina Hayden carries out a Welcome to Country
Children watch a welcome to country ceremony

items.title: Heat-related deaths 'tip of the iceberg' in India as UN sends El Niño warning

children.content: India's deadly heatwave comes as the UN warns the world to prepare for a moderate to strong El Niño this year.

image.altText: A man puts his head under a running outdoor tap.

items.title: Rocket motor test success marks Australian-made missile milestone

children.content: In what is being described as a significant leap forward in Australia's push to develop and manufacture its own missiles, newly released footage shows the first test-firing of a locally made solid rocket motor.

image.altText: A trail of fire appearing out of a rocket motor as it is shot.

items.title: These companies have controversial pasts. Google AI didn't tell us

children.content: We searched Google for information on some of the world's biggest tobacco companies. The results raised questions about how much of the AI overview was coming from the brands themselves.

image.altText: A hand holding a phone where the user has googled tobacco company Philip Morris.

items.title: WA farmer braces for 'year of survival' as farm profits dive and costs bite

children.content: The ABARES report for the June quarter predicts national crop production will fall by more than 20 per cent, with profits down 70 per cent for broadacre farms.

items.title: Semaglutide is used for weight loss but may benefit kidneys and heart

children.content: Research shows semaglutide — commonly sold as Ozempic or Wegovy — can improve kidney function and survival rates for people with some chronic diseases, even if they have heart issues.

gallery.altText: Two boxes of medication side by side, labelled Ozempic and Wegovy

thumbnail.altText: Two boxes of medication side by side, labelled Ozempic and Wegovy
